PATIENT_ID: TEST_SPINAL_TIMING_NC_001
ARRIVAL_TIME: 2026-01-15 06:00:00

[TRAUMA_HP] 2026-01-15 06:10:00
Category I activation for 78-year-old female. Ground-level fall.
Blunt trauma mechanism. Neck pain and bilateral upper extremity paresthesias.
Motor and sensory examination documented.
Neurologic deficit: bilateral upper extremity weakness and sensory loss.
BP 152/84, HR 78, RR 18, GCS 15.

[IMAGING] 2026-01-15 11:30:00
CT cervical spine: C5 vertebral body avulsion fracture with transdiscal
fracture extending through C5-C6 disc space. Anterior and posterior
longitudinal ligament disruption. C4-C5 spinous process fractures.
Spinal fracture confirmed on CT imaging. Unstable cervical fracture pattern.

[PHYSICIAN_NOTE] 2026-01-15 14:00:00
Unstable cervical fracture. Neurosurgery consulted for surgical planning.
Cervical collar maintained. Patient hemodynamically stable.
Surgical stabilization recommended.

[OPERATIVE_NOTE] 2026-01-19 09:00:00
Procedure: C5-C6 ACDF (anterior cervical discectomy and fusion).
Decompression and fusion performed for unstable cervical fracture.
Instrumentation placed. Patient tolerated procedure without complication.
